title: pellaea atropurpurea var. mucronata
intro: Pellaea atropurpurea var. mucronata, also known as the purple-leafed cliff brake, is a species of fern native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pellaea atropurpurea var. mucronata include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by drying the fronds and using them in teas, infusions, or as a topical application.
